title: Niphobolus polycarpon
taxonomy: Niphobolus polycarpon is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of fern that is native to tropical regions of Asia. The classification of this plant is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, phylum - Pteridophyta, class - Polypodiopsida, order - Polypodiales, family - Dryopteridaceae, genus - Niphobolus, species - N. polycarpon. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns that are also known as wood ferns. Niphobolus polycarpon is a part of this family, which consists of around 1,200 species of ferns.
uses: Niphobolus polycarpon, also known as the Indian pennywort or small-leaved Niphobolus, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for various purposes. It is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of cold and flu. Additionally, it is believed to have wound-healing properties and is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant's leaves and roots are used in herbal remedies, often in combination with other medicinal plants. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Niphobolus polycarpon for medicinal purposes.

Research has identified several specific flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as the alkaloid aucubin. intro: Niphobolus polycarpon, also known as the Indian spikenard, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly found in the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Niphobolus polycarpon include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Niphobolus polycarpon is often used to prepare herbal decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including arthritis and bronchitis.
